commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
Say. The Israelites sing this mournful canticle to ver. 7., which the prophet composes for them, to show the certainty of the event. It is very difficult. (Calmet)
The whole synagogue speaks. (Menochius)
Depart. How do you pretend to say that the Assyrian is departing, when indeed he is coming to divide our lands amongst his subjects? (Challoner)
The Cutheans were sent into the country, 4 Kings xvii. 24. (Calmet)
Septuagint, "and there was none to hinder him from returning, our lands were divided. "(Haydock)

commentaries.author: Richard Challoner
commentaries.content: How shall he depart: How do you pretend to say that the Assyrian is departing; when indeed he is coming to divide our lands amongst his subjects?

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
